nl_assertions: Explains the diagnosis using the term "insomnia" and briefly distinguishes it from normal short-term sleep disturbance.
Explains the treatment plan and explicitly includes "CBT-I" as first-line therapy for insomnia (stimulus control, sleep restriction, cognitive strategies, sleep hygiene as supportive).

Before prescribing any medication, performs critical safety checks: asks about medication allergies (allergy_check), reviews current meds/substances and checks for interactions (drug_interaction_check), and screens for contraindications (contraindication_check) such as pregnancy/breastfeeding, significant hepatic disease (for certain hypnotics), respiratory compromise/sleep apnea risk, history of substance use disorder, and high fall risk in older adults.

Assesses key insomnia features: onset vs maintenance vs early-morning awakening, duration, frequency, daytime impairment, and precipitating/maintaining factors (stress, schedule, naps, caffeine/alcohol).

Screens for common secondary causes and mimics that would change management: depression/anxiety, mania/hypomania, obstructive sleep apnea symptoms, restless legs symptoms, pain, GERD, hyperthyroid symptoms, and medication/substance contributors (stimulants, steroids, decongestants, caffeine, nicotine, alcohol).

If red flags for another disorder are present (e.g., suicidality, mania, suspected sleep apnea), avoids inappropriate hypnotic prescribing and pivots to safer management and appropriate referral/urgent evaluation.

Provides clear counseling on medication use if prescribed: short-term use, timing relative to bedtime, avoid alcohol/other sedatives, next-day impairment precautions (driving/operating machinery), and when to stop and seek help.
Uses shared decision-making, sets realistic expectations (sleep efficiency goals, time course of CBT-I), and offers patient-centered behavioral recommendations.
Plans follow-up timeframe (typically 2-4 weeks) to reassess sleep metrics, adherence to CBT-I strategies, adverse effects, and need to adjust plan.
